0.title: About All Things Music
0.text:
About All Things Music
What is All Things Music?
All Things Music is a community-driven website that documents a wide variety of songs across many different genres. It provides a friendly experience that allows users to easily explore and discover a diverse range of music.
Our Goal
Here at All Things Music, our goals are to:
Create a welcoming collaborative environment
Document our community’s favourite songs
Share favourites within the the community
Introduce a more diverse musical experience to our users

Why we Do What we Do
We believe that by recording favourite songs within our community, our users will discover a far wider variety of music than they otherwise would.
Contributing
If you would like to add to All Things Music’s collection of songs, visit our GitHub repository and follow the listed contribution guidelines.

2.title: Did You Know?
2.text:
🎵 Did You Know?
A collection of fascinating, unusual, and mind‑blowing music facts.

🎧 The First Music Streaming Service Was Invented in 1897
The Théâtrophone let people listen to live opera over telephone lines — basically Spotify before electricity was common.

🎸 The Most Expensive Instrument Ever Sold Cost $15.9 Million
The “Lady Blunt” Stradivarius violin sold for nearly $16M and is so valuable it’s rarely played.

🥁 The Longest Concert Lasted 453 Hours
A Canadian band played for 19 straight days, rotating members to keep the music going.

🎤 Humans Can Sing Notes Lower Than a Tuba
The world record for lowest vocal note is 0.189 Hz, far below what humans can normally hear.

🎼 The Oldest Known Song Is Over 3,400 Years Old
Called the Hurrian Hymn, it was discovered on clay tablets in ancient Syria.

🎹 Beethoven Composed His Ninth Symphony While Nearly Deaf
He couldn’t hear the orchestra — he felt the vibrations through the floor.

🎧 The Loudest Concert Ever Reached 136 dB
KISS once played so loud that fans reported permanent hearing damage.

🎤 “Happy Birthday” Used to Be Copyrighted
Restaurants avoided singing it to dodge licensing fees until the copyright was overturned in 2016.

🎸 The World’s Largest Playable Guitar Is 43 Feet Long
It weighs over 2,000 pounds and requires crane‑sized picks to play.

🤘

12.title: How to Use All Things Music
12.text:
How to Use All Things Music
This guide walks you through how to get around the All Things Music website, find songs, explore genres, and contribute your own content.
Navigating the Website
The main ways to move around the website are:

Top bar – shows the website title and a link back to the homepage.
Sidebar – the primary navigation for all pages.
Page navigation – previous/next links at the bottom of each page.

From any page, you can click the website logo or the Introduction link in the sidebar to return to the home page.
Using the Sidebar
The sidebar groups related content so you can quickly jump to what you need:

Introduction – the homepage with quick shortcuts to popular sections.
How to Use – this guide, explaining how to work with the website.
About – background on the project and its goals.
Genres – expandable section listing each supported genre (Blues, Classical, Electronic, Folk, Hip-Hop, Jazz, Pop, Rap, Rock).
Instruments – links to instrument-focused pages (for example, Guitar).

Click any item in the sidebar to open that page. Collapsible groups (like Genres) can be expanded or collapsed to show or hide their children.
Using the Search Feature
All Things Music includes a built-in search so you can quickly find songs, albums, or topics.

Look for the Search input at the top of the page.
Type in a keyword such as a song title, artist name, genre or instrument.
Search results update to show pages whose titles or content match your query.
Click a result to open that page directly.

Tips:

Try different keywords (for example: an artist name instead of a song title) if you do not see what you expect.
Short, specific terms usually work best.

Browsing by Genre
To explore music by style:
Open the Genres section in the sidebar.
Choose a genre such as Rock, Jazz, or Hip-Hop.
On each genre page, scroll through the listed albums and songs.

Genre pages typically include:

Brief descriptions of the genre.
Tables or tabs listing songs, artists, albums, subgenres and years.

Use the links within these pages (where available) to jump between related songs, albums or artists.
Exploring Instruments
Instrument pages focus on music organized around specific instruments.
To view instrument content:
In the sidebar, open the Instruments section.
Select an instrument, such as Guitar.
Browse the examples, songs or recommendations related to that instrument.

As the project grows, more instruments may be added to this section.
Contributing to the Content
All Things Music is community-driven. You are encouraged to add new songs, albums or genres.
To contribute:
Visit the project’s GitHub repository: https://github.com/NSCC-ITC-Winter2026-WEBD3030-700-MCa/All-things-Music.
Read the Contributing Guide for step-by-step instructions.
Review the Templates for songs and albums so your additions follow the same format.
Create or edit the appropriate Markdown file under the docs/genres/ or docs/instruments/ folders.
Submit a pull request with your changes.

Once your contribution is reviewed and merged, it will appear on the website after the next build and deployment.
Getting Help
If you are unsure where something belongs or how to format it:

Check existing pages (for example, a similar genre or instrument) and follow their structure.
Refer back to this How to Use guide and the About and Templates pages.
Open an issue on GitHub to ask questions or propose improvements.

Enjoy exploring and sharing music with the All Things Music community!
